title: Eccoilopus formosanus
intro: There is no information available on Eccoilopus formosanus.
taxonomy: Eccoilopus formosanus is classified in the family Pinaceae, which includes other coniferous trees. This species belongs to the genus Eccoilopus, a group of trees native to East Asia. More specifically, Eccoilopus formosanus is classified as Eccoilopus formosanus, a species within the genus Eccoilopus. However, more research is needed to confirm the exact classification of this species. Further classification details are also available under the broader category of Pinaceae and the phylum Coniferophyta.
benefits: Eccoilopus formosanus, also known as the Formosan fir, has been found to have various health benefits due to its bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The essential oils extracted from the Eccoilopus formosanus have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of certain diseases. Additionally, the plant's bioactive compounds may have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, contributing to its potential health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits and potential applications in medicine.
constituents: Eccoilopus formosanus, also known as the Formosan sweet cicely, is a plant species that contains a variety of active constituents. These constituents include furanocoumarins, which are known for their phototoxic properties, as well as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in these compounds, which may contribute to its potential medicinal properties.
